name: add-gcal-tool
tagline: Wires Google Calendar into NanoClaw as an MCP tool via OneCLI-managed OAuth stubs, mirroring the sibling Gmail-tool pattern.
verdict: Solid, security-conscious infra skill: never writes real credentials (only OneCLI placeholder stubs, chmod 600), pins the MCP package version, checks re-run/idempotency at every mutating step, and has an unusually thorough failure-diagnosis table. Weak spots are purely hygiene/quality: no NOT-for scope guidance so it could misfire against sibling add-*-tool.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for'/exclusion text anywhere; sibling skills add-gmail-tool/add-github could plausibly be confused with this one.
No version field, changelog section, or dated revision notes anywhere in SKILL.md.
